# Compile Constraints

Tests for compiling dependencies with constraints.

## Basic constraints

### Constraints file

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_txt -->

Compile dependencies with a constraints file.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
anyio==3.7.0
```

```toml constraints.txt
idna<3.4
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
anyio==3.7.0
    # via -r requirements.in
idna==3.3
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   anyio
sniffio==1.3.1
    # via anyio

----- stderr -----
Resolved 3 packages in [TIME]
```

### Constraints with markers

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_markers -->

Compile dependencies with constraints that use markers.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
anyio
```

```toml constraints.txt
sniffio==1.2.0;python_version<='3.7'
sniffio==1.3.0;python_version>'3.7'
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
anyio==4.3.0
    # via -r requirements.in
idna==3.6
    # via anyio
sniffio==1.3.0
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   anyio

----- stderr -----
Resolved 3 packages in [TIME]
```

### Constraint with extra

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraint_extra -->

Constraints with extras constrain the version but don't include the extra.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
flask
```

```toml constraints.txt
flask[dotenv]<24.3.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
blinker==1.7.0
    # via flask
click==8.1.7
    # via flask
flask==3.0.2
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   -r requirements.in
itsdangerous==2.1.2
    # via flask
jinja2==3.1.3
    # via flask
markupsafe==2.1.5
    # via
    #   jinja2
    #   werkzeug
werkzeug==3.0.1
    # via flask

----- stderr -----
Resolved 7 packages in [TIME]
```

### Constraint URL overridden by version

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::requirement_constraint_override_url -->

When a requirement and constraint both specify URLs, but an override specifies a version, the
resolution fails because the version doesn't exist at the URL.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
anyio @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/db/4d/3970183622f0330d3c23d9b8a5f52e365e50381fd484d08e3285104333d3/anyio-4.3.0.tar.gz
```

```toml constraints.txt
anyio @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/14/fd/2f20c40b45e4fb4324834aea24bd4afdf1143390242c0b33774da0e2e34f/anyio-4.3.0-py3-none-any.whl
```

```toml overrides.txt
anyio==3.7.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt --override overrides.txt
success: false
exit_code: 1
----- stdout -----

----- stderr -----
  × No solution found when resolving dependencies:
  ╰─▶ Because there is no version of anyio==3.7.0 and you require anyio==3.7.0, we can conclude that your requirements are unsatisfiable.
```

### Compatible version constraint

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_compatible_version -->

Resolve with a constraint that pins a transitive dependency to a compatible version.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
virtualenv
```

```toml constraints.txt
filelock==3.8.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
distlib==0.3.8
    # via virtualenv
filelock==3.8.0
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   virtualenv
platformdirs==3.11.0
    # via virtualenv
virtualenv==20.21.1
    # via -r requirements.in

----- stderr -----
Resolved 4 packages in [TIME]
```

### Incompatible version constraint

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_incompatible_version -->

Resolve with a constraint that pins a direct dependency to an incompatible version.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
filelock==1.0.0
```

```toml constraints.txt
filelock==3.8.0
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: false
exit_code: 1
----- stdout -----

----- stderr -----
  × No solution found when resolving dependencies:
  ╰─▶ Because you require filelock==1.0.0 and filelock==3.8.0, we can conclude that your requirements are unsatisfiable.
```

### Constraint with compatible URL

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_compatible_url -->

A constraint can specify a URL that is compatible with the version requirement.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
anyio>4
```

```toml constraints.txt
anyio @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/cd/d6d9bb1dadf73e7af02d18225cbd2c93f8552e13130484f1c8dcfece292b/anyio-4.2.0-py3-none-any.whl
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
anyio @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/cd/d6d9bb1dadf73e7af02d18225cbd2c93f8552e13130484f1c8dcfece292b/anyio-4.2.0-py3-none-any.whl
    # via
    #   -c constraints.txt
    #   -r requirements.in
idna==3.6
    # via anyio
sniffio==1.3.1
    # via anyio

----- stderr -----
Resolved 3 packages in [TIME]
```

### Constraint with incompatible URL

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::compile_constraints_incompatible_url -->

A constraint specifying a URL that is incompatible with the version requirement fails.

```toml
# file: requirements.in
anyio<4
```

```toml constraints.txt
anyio @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/bf/cd/d6d9bb1dadf73e7af02d18225cbd2c93f8552e13130484f1c8dcfece292b/anyio-4.2.0-py3-none-any.whl
```

```console
$ uv pip compile requirements.in --constraint constraints.txt
success: false
exit_code: 1
----- stdout -----

----- stderr -----
  × No solution found when resolving dependencies:
  ╰─▶ Because only anyio>=4 is available and you require anyio<4, we can conclude that your requirements are unsatisfiable.
```

### Constraint from pyproject.toml

<!-- from pip_compile.rs::constraint_dependency_from_pyproject -->

Constraints can be specified in pyproject.toml using `tool.uv.constraint-dependencies`.

```toml
# file: pyproject.toml
[project]
name = "example"
version = "0.0.0"
dependencies = [
  "anyio==3.7.0"
]

[tool.uv]
constraint-dependencies = [
  "idna<3.4"
]
```

```console
$ uv pip compile pyproject.toml
success: true
exit_code: 0
----- stdout -----
# This file was autogenerated by uv via the following command:
#    uv --cache-dir [CACHE_DIR] pip compile pyproject.toml
anyio==3.7.0
    # via example (pyproject.toml)
idna==3.3
    # via
    #   -c (workspace)
    #   anyio
sniffio==1.3.1
    # via anyio

----- stderr -----
Resolved 3 packages in [TIME]
```
